findings.claim: SOTA verified is blocked unless numeric dominance is causally tied to the real client implementation and cannot come from benchmark-only paths or curl startup/pooling artifacts.
findings.counterexample: All current required outcomes and result paths can pass, including O-sota-threshold-matrix, while SOTA-F2/SOTA-F4 remain false because no required outcome/result path demands profiling, ablation, or proof that the benchmark exercises the real production client path instead of a benchmark-only fast path.
findings.suggested_repair: Add a required outcome/result path for causal performance and no-benchmark-only-path evidence, require profiling or ablation plus proof that benchmark traffic uses the real client path and same pooling semantics, add it to all_required and sota_verified_requires, and add counterexample checks for benchmark-only paths and unexplained startup/pooling artifacts.

smallest_counterexample: All listed evidence/subgoals can be true while the required causal/no-benchmark-only SOTA frontier gate remains false.

repair_applied.summary: Added O-causal-performance-evidence, RP-real-client-causal-performance, completion logic requirements, no-offset rules, and counterexample checks for real-client-path and causal profiling/tracing/ablation evidence.

review_summary: The second SOTA-definition counterexample gate failed. The numeric threshold repair closed the prior gap, but SOTA-F2/SOTA-F4 were still not executable. The repair adds causal performance and real-client-path evidence as required completion objects.
